stories.title: U.K. convenes 40 nations to discuss Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Foreign ministers from more than 40 countries are meeting virtually to discuss President Trump's request for help in ensuring shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
stories.description: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

stories.title: Hegseth ousts the Army chief of staff as the Iran war rages through Week 5
stories.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Army Chief of Staff Randy George to step down and retire, a U.S. official confirmed to NPR.

stories.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

stories.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
stories.description: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

stories.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

stories.title: A Turkish border town known for its cats - in times of peace
stories.description: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers - and its cats.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
stories.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
stories.description: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

stories.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
stories.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: ‘Disturbing trend’ of US-Israeli strikes hitting non-military targets in Iran
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran early Friday, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says there’s been a “disturbing trend” in recent days of US-Israeli strikes hitting non-military targets across Iran. A strike on Iran’s tallest bridge last night killed eight people and wounded 90 others, said Sayah. The...

stories.title: Middle East war live: Iran warns UN Security Council against ‘provocative action’ on Hormuz
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Friday warned against any "provocative action" ahead of an expected UN Security Council vote on the use of force to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Backed by the US, the measure comes amid rising tensions in the region and concerns over glo...

stories.title: Russia's military campaign in Ukraine stalls for first time in more than two years
stories.description: Russia’s advance in Ukraine stalled in March, with no territorial gains for the first time in over two years, according to the Institute for the Study of War. Ukrainian forces recaptured small areas, signalling a slowdown in Moscow’s momentum after months of progress, raising new...

stories.title: Burkina army, jihadists killed more than 1,800 civilians since 2023
stories.description: In tonight's edition, Human Rights Watch says Burkina Faso’s government forces and jihadists together killed more than 1800 civilians between 2023 and 2025. Also, a collapsed, unfinished school in Accra kills four people and injures nearly 30, raising concerns over unsafe classro...

stories.title: Fact-checking Trump's address on the war in Iran
stories.description: In his 19-minute address to the nation from the White House on Wednesday, President Donald Trump defended his war in Iran, which is now entering his second month, presenting the operation as a success that he said was “nearing completion.” But the President also stretched the tru...

stories.title: Macron says Trump marriage jibe does not 'merit response'
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron said on Thursday Donald Trump's mocking comments about his marriage did not merit a response, calling them "neither elegant nor appropriate" for the situation. But after days of tensions over US-Israeli strikes on Iran and over NATO, an apparently...

stories.title: Europe, NATO under pressure over Iran war: 'Trump strong with the weak and weak with the strong'
stories.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Hélène Conway-Mouret, French Senator, Vice Chair of the Foreign Affairs Committee. According to Conway-Mouret, Donald Trump’s Iran war strategy is creating incredible volatility for European security, transatlantic relations, and even domesti...

stories.title: Israel's Lebanon endgame: 'Dismantle Hezbollah entirely as a political and social movement'
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Yezid Sayigh, a senior fellow at the Malcolm H. Kerr Carnegie Middle East Center in Beirut. He sees Israel’s military actions in southern Lebanon within a broader political and strategic framework that extends beyond immediate battlefield object...

stories.title: US and Israel 'again bombing what were clearly non-military sites' in Iran
stories.description: Reporting from Tehran for FRANCE 24, Reza Sayah says that "today again US and Israeli forces bombed what were clearly non-military sites in broad daylight and later in the day US president Donald Trump seemed to boast about it"

stories.title: Iran condemns US-Israeli ‘moral collapse’ after attacks on civilian sites
stories.description: Strikes damage or destroy Iran’s tallest bridge and a century-old medical research centre fighting diseases.

stories.title: Iranian women footballers reveal ‘enormous pressure’ of the Asian Cup saga
stories.description: Two Iranian players who sought asylum in Australia before changing their minds tell their story to Al Jazeera.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 35 of US-Israeli attacks?
stories.description: The US and Israel have hit a century-old medical research centre and a bridge near Tehran.

stories.title: How China’s ‘teapot’ refineries are cushioning it from Iran war oil crisis
stories.description: Small independent refineries are helping China continue importing oil from Iran.

stories.title: Iran war live: Ex-FM Zarif says Tehran should ‘declare victory, make deal’
stories.description: Trump meanwhile steps up threats after US strike on Iran's largest bridge, as Tehran launches attacks across the region.

stories.title: How war on Iran turned Pakistan’s LNG surplus into a looming shortage
stories.description: Pakistan braces for gas shortage crisis as LNG supplies collapse amid escalating regional conflict.

stories.title: Iran vows retaliation after deadly US strike on bridge in Karaj
stories.description: Iranian officials say the country’s largest bridge will be “built back stronger,” but America’s standing won't recover.

stories.title: Oil shock triggers global price spikes as Iran war drags on
stories.description: Fuel shocks from the US-Israel war on Iran are rippling worldwide, as Strait of Hormuz disruptions push prices higher.

stories.title: Iran says army forces are ready to repel US-Israeli attacks
stories.description: Iran says it is ready to counter any US-Israeli attacks, insisting its military capabilities remain intact.

stories.title: As war drags on, US reiterates Trump open to diplomacy with Iran
stories.description: US State Department accuses Iran of attacking civilian sites as Trump shares footage of destruction of Iranian bridge.

stories.title: Pentagon shake-up as Hegseth ousts army chief amid Iran conflict
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has abruptly forced Army Chief of Staff General Randy George into immediate retirement amid the ongoing war with Iran, multiple US media outlets reported on Friday. The outlets described the move as one of the most consequential Penta...

stories.title: US blows up key Iranian bridge, steel plants
stories.description: • Trump says more will follow after earlier threatening to bomb Iran back to ‘Stone Ages’, claims war nearly over• Tehran vows ‘crushing’ attacks on US after Trump threats• Iran launches missiles towards Israel; fire near Mashhad airport after projectile hits fuel tank• Dozens of...

stories.title: Gulf states seek UN mandate for force to protect Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) on Thursday called for the UN Security Council to authorise the use of force to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian attacks. Iran has placed a stranglehold on the key shipping lane — threatening fuel supplies and b...

stories.title: Doomsday Clock chief points to risk of nuclear weapons use in Iran conflict
stories.description: According to John Mecklin, editor-in-chief of the Bulletin of the Atomic Scientists, there is currently a "low probability" that the US or Israel could use nuclear weapons on Iran

stories.title: Iran reports shooting down US F-35 fighter jet
stories.description: The Iranian military specified that the likelihood that the crew had managed to eject was low

stories.title: Oil refinery in Kuwait on fire after Iranian drone attack
stories.description: According to available information, there were no casualties

stories.title: More bridges, power stations to be destroyed in Iran — US President Trump
stories.description: Donald Trump stressed that Iran has should go for a deal with the United States

stories.title: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov to meet Egyptian counterpart Abdelatty
stories.description: The main issue on the ministers' agenda is expected to focus on the escalation of the Iranian conflict

stories.title: Explosions reported in Iran’s southern city of Bushehr — TV
stories.description: According to TV channel, missile launch systems were shelled

stories.title: Europe’s largest airliner opts to cancel 10% of its flights due to lack of fuel
stories.description: Chief Executive of Ryanair Michael O’Leary blamed the announced air traffic disruptions on the United States that jointly with Israel had launched a military operation against Iran

stories.title: US-Israeli strikes on civilian facilities will not force Iranians to surrender — Araghchi
stories.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi emphasized that "every bridge and building will be built back stronger"

stories.title: Yemen’s Houthis claim missile attack on targets in Tel Aviv
stories.description: The movement’s military spokesman Yahya Saree also indicated the Houthis "will act according to further developments, based on escalation or de-escalation by the enemy"

stories.title: Death toll from strike on bridge in Iran rises to eight — authorities
stories.description: 95 people were injured

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz to be closed to US, Israel for long time — Iranian General Staff
stories.description: A representative of the Iranian armed forces Abolfazl Shekarchi stressed that "under no pretext will they approach it"

stories.title: Iran targets Oracle data center in Dubai — IRGC
stories.description: Earlier on Thursday, Iran’s elite army unit claimed to have carried out strikes on a cloud computing center of US tech firm Amazon in Bahrain

stories.title: Iran can't have unilateral control over Hormuz - Arab League secretary general
stories.description: In the opinion of Ahmed Aboul Gheit, "the draft document on freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz proposed by the Gulf Cooperation Council should be supported"

stories.title: Top diplomats of Russia, Iran discuss situation in Middle East over phone — Russian MFA
stories.description: Sergey Lavrov and Abbas Araghchi also exchanged opinions on the course of debate at the UN Security Council regarding ways to ensure the safe passage of vessels in the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Iran destroys US bases in Middle East, says Houthi leader
stories.description: Abdul Malik al-Houthi is convinced that the Iranian operations were accompanied by "heavy losses among officers and soldiers" of the armies of the United States and Israel

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
